nodes.bodyText:
Skip secondary adapter when primary auto-run already succeeds
runAutoSwitchFlow always invokes config.secondary after the primary loop, even when the primary returns success or needs_input. In those cases this creates an unnecessary switch and can downgrade a successful primary run into a blocked/error overall result if the secondary invocation fails, which violates the documented auto-switch trigger behavior (switch only on exhausted/approval/retry-failure paths).
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Report terminal adapter errors as error, not blocked
The flow status collapses every non-success terminal adapter outcome into blocked, so true execution failures from the final adapter are mislabeled and downstream logic cannot distinguish recoverable approval blocks from hard errors. This is especially problematic for telemetry/retry handling when the secondary returns status: "error".
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.

Parse JSONL adapter output before status normalization
The Codex invocation is configured with --stdio-jsonl, but parsePayload attempts JSON.parse(stdout) on the entire buffer. Any multi-line JSONL output will fail parsing, causing status inference to fall back to exit code and potentially mark runs as success while dropping structured status/parsed_evidence fields needed for switch decisions.
Useful? React with 👍 / 👎.
